rationale_for_baseline_tier: Cellulitis with a systemic inflammatory response (fever, rigors, tachycardia) needs urgent same-day in-person assessment for antibiotics (potentially IV) and sepsis evaluation; a telehealth provider cannot examine for necrotising features or mark the border — hence T4, escalating to T5 for necrotising/sepsis features.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Worsening systemic features: high fever, confusion, dizziness on standing, or vomiting/unable to keep antibiotics down
escalation_edges.rationale: Indicates sepsis requiring IV antibiotics and resuscitation.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If your fever worsens, you feel confused or dizzy when standing, or you can't keep the antibiotics down — call 000 or go to the emergency department now.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Necrotising features: pain far out of proportion, purple/black blisters, crackling under the skin, numb patches, or very rapid spread past a marked line
escalation_edges.rationale: Suggests necrotising fasciitis — a surgical emergency.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If the pain becomes far worse than the skin looks, you see purple or black blisters, feel crackling under the skin or numb patches, or the redness races up your leg — call 000 immediately.

contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Partner can help monitor the border/fever and arrange urgent transport if deteriorating.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: You need to be seen in person today for antibiotics because you have a fever and chills with a spreading leg infection. Keep the leg raised above heart level. Mark or photograph the edge of the redness to track it.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: Same-day in-person/urgent care or ED for antibiotics + assessment; 000 for sepsis/necrotising features
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: Attend an after-hours service or ED tonight given the fever; 000 for the emergency features above.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Document febrile spreading lower-limb cellulitis from an insect-bite portal, systemic response, antibiotics commenced, border marked, necrotising features screened negative.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Home antibiotics/routine follow-up for febrile spreading cellulitis without in-person assessment or a necrotising/sepsis screen.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: Sepsis or an unrecognised necrotising fasciitis progresses before the patient is assessed — limb loss or death.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: Urgent same-day GP without ED-level assessment despite systemic toxicity.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: Ambulance for uncomplicated cellulitis without systemic toxicity or necrotising features (here systemic features are present, so escalation is appropriate).

clinical_uncertainty_handling.key_uncertainty: Cannot palpate for crepitus/pain-out-of-proportion, mark the border, or objectively assess for sepsis remotely.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.correct_response_to_uncertainty: Arrange urgent same-day in-person assessment for antibiotics and sepsis screening, advise elevation, and safety-net for necrotising fasciitis.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.wrong_response_to_uncertainty: Prescribing remotely with routine follow-up, or advising dependency, or omitting the necrotising-fasciitis screen.
